message:
fix(malloc-abi): track raw_alloc/raw_free in fallback table for leak/UAF accounting

`raw_alloc` and `raw_free` were the only allocator entry points that
bypassed the `fallback_insert` / `fallback_remove` accounting on the
native host-resolution path. That made internally-allocated buffers
invisible to the leak-detection / UAF-adversarial harnesses, which use
the fallback table as the canonical "live allocations" registry.

Wire them up:

- `raw_alloc(size)`: capture the pointer returned from
  `native_libc_malloc`, run `fallback_insert(ptr)` so it shows up in
  the live registry, and return the pointer unchanged.
- `raw_free(ptr)`: discard the pointer's entry via `fallback_remove`
  before calling `native_libc_free`, so the registry stays consistent
  with reentry-protected drops.

No behavior change for callers; only the bookkeeping side-effect on
the fallback table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
